What is the time zone of Salt Lake City? Salt Lake City, the capital and most populous city of Utah, is located in the Mountain Time Zone. This time zone is typically denoted as Mountain Standard Time (MST) during the standard time period and Mountain Daylight Time (MDT) during daylight saving time. When daylight saving time is in effect, from early spring to late fall, Salt Lake City observes MDT, which is 6 hours behind Coordinated Universal Time (UTC-6). During the rest of the year, the city follows MST, which is 7 hours behind UTC (UTC-7).
